question.text: Scenario: Sarvottam Fintech Ltd. (SFL), an unlisted fintech company, plans to launch its IPO. The company's financials for the last three years show: net tangible assets of Rs.5 crore, Rs.4.2 crore, and Rs.3.8 crore; average operating profit of Rs.17 crore (positive each year); and net worth of Rs.2 crore each year. The IPO is structured as a book building issue with a price band of Rs.280-Rs.320 per share. The total issue size is 50 lakh shares, comprising 35 lakh fresh issue shares and 15 lakh shares as Offer for Sale by the promoter, Harish Mehta. The issue receives the following subscription on closing day (T day): QIB category -- 1.5 crore shares bid (against 25 lakh shares reserved); NII category -- 60 lakh shares bid (against 7.5 lakh shares reserved); RII category -- 52.5 lakh shares bid (against 17.5 lakh shares reserved). The cut-off price is determined at Rs.310 per share. Pooja Sharma, a retail investor, applied for 200 shares at cut-off price through ASBA, with funds blocked in her SCSB account. Vikram Desai, the RTA officer, is coordinating the post-issue process. Question 3 of 5: Pooja applied for 200 shares at the cut-off price of Rs.310 through ASBA. If the RII category is oversubscribed 3 times and allotment is proportionate, approximately how many shares will Pooja be allotted, and what happens to the remaining blocked amount?

answer.text: 200 shares allotted; entire blocked amount of Rs.62,000 debited
Approximately 67 shares allotted; Rs.20,770 debited and Rs.41,230 unblocked
100 shares allotted; Rs.31,000 debited and Rs.31,000 refunded by cheque
No shares allotted; full Rs.62,000 refunded within 15 days

explanation.text: **The Logic:** RII oversubscription = 3x, so proportionate allotment = 200/3 ≈ 67 shares (rounded). Blocked amount = 200 x Rs.310 = Rs.62,000. Allotment debit = 67 x Rs.310 = Rs.20,770. Unblocked = Rs.62,000 - Rs.20,770 = Rs.41,230. Under ASBA, the unallotted portion is unblocked (not refunded by cheque) -- the money was never debited, only blocked. **The Trap:** Under ASBA, there is no refund by cheque. The blocked amount for unallotted shares is simply unblocked. Candidates who think of the pre-ASBA refund mechanism will choose the wrong answer. Also, 100 shares would be the allotment at 2x oversubscription, not 3x. **Cross-Exam Context:** NISM Series II-A Chapter 10.3.1 explains ASBA blocking/unblocking. The RTA instructs the SCSB's controlling branch for debit/unblock. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** At 3x oversubscription, Pooja gets roughly one-third of what she applied for. Help clients plan their application size based on expected oversubscription. If they want 60-70 shares, applying for 200 at 3x oversubscription is the right strategy.
